orchestkit · #2947 · pretool/bash/display-lint

One command. Seven stages. Denied.

display-lint blocked long compound one-liners by counting shell stages with normalizeCommand(), a security primitive whose documented job is to "prevent bypass via quoting, escapes, and compound operators". It strips quotes but keeps their contents, and rewrites an escaped \| into a real pipe, so an rm hidden in quotes still matches. Correct for the danger blocker. Exactly backwards for a cosmetic one.

The tell: a quoted string is opaque payload to the shell, never a stage separator. Paste any command below. The left column is the shipped counter; the right is the shell's own reading. Chips outlined in red are stages that do not exist.
Bash command
length 0 chars gate fires above 200 chars and 3+ stages

Shipped deny

0stages

Fixed pass

0stages
Ask Claude to explain this verdict
Also changed: the hook no longer denies at all. Blocking correct work over a cosmetic concern taught users to reach for ORK_DISPLAY_LINT=0, which disables the gate wholesale — the exact outcome it exists to prevent. It now warns and the command runs. Genuinely cluttered chains are still flagged, just never blocked.
#2947 · sibling defect in the danger blocker: #2955 (|| read as a pipe)
